title: Magical Grilled Peruvian Shrimp Skewers
description: A briny and velvety Peruvian dish featuring coconut flakes and grilled to perfection, this recipe is an adventurous twist on traditional shrimp skewers, served fondue-style for a fun and interactive meal.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together lime juice, garlic, ginger, ají amarillo pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the shrimp to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated shrimp onto skewers, leaving a small space between each shrimp.
Brush the grill with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the shrimp skewers for 2-3 minutes per side, or until they are pink and cooked through.
While the shrimp are cooking, sprinkle coconut flakes onto a plate or tray.
Once the shrimp are cooked, remove them from the grill and roll them in the coconut flakes to coat.
Serve the shrimp skewers in a fondue pot with your favorite dipping sauces.
Garnish with additional coconut flakes and lime wedges, if desired.
